page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.e3.medium is a memory-optimized virtual server instance running on the KVM hypervisor. It features 2 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 2 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8269CY processor clocked at 2.5 GHz. The architecture is x86_64, with a cache hierarchy consisting of 32 KB L1d cache, 1.0 MB L2 cache, and 35 MB L3 cache. The instance is equipped with 16.0 GB of system memory, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware.

Performance benchmarks place this instance in the lower tiers for compute and memory throughput. The stress-ng single-core CPU performance score is 1102.08, and the multi-core score is 2162.17, both falling into the bottom 25% and 10% respectively. Memory bandwidth tests show 156.12 GB/sec for cached small-block reads, but uncached large-block reads drop to 17.2 GB/sec, and large-block writes reach 14.52 GB/sec. PassMark CPU and memory scores are 2616.1 and 1339.64, indicating limited raw processing power. Cryptographic and compression tasks also show lower-tier performance, with a PassMark encryption score of 812.86 and single-threaded gzip compression at 18.89 GB/sec.

Given its dedicated CPU allocation and high memory density relative to its compute resources, the ecs.e3.medium offers a qualitative cost-efficiency profile suited for memory-bound workloads that do not require intense CPU processing. It is appropriate for lightweight database operations, caching layers such as Redis (which achieves 2.52 million operations per second in testing), and basic web serving with a static throughput of 2.49 GB/sec. However, it is not suitable for compute-intensive tasks, heavy data compilation, or storage-heavy applications due to the lack of local storage.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.e3.medium is a memory-optimized virtual server instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 2 dedicated vCPUs on an Intel Xeon 8269CY x86_64 processor clocked at 2.5 GHz, paired with 16.0 GB of RAM, yielding a high 8.0 GB of memory per core. The instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Benchmark results indicate modest compute and memory performance, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 2162.17 and uncached large-block memory read bandwidth of 17.2 GB/sec. It achieves 2.52 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks and 2.49 GB/sec in static web serving. This hardware profile is qualitatively cost-effective for memory-intensive but compute-light workloads, such as caching layers, lightweight database operations, and basic web hosting.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.e3.medium is a memory-optimized virtual server featuring 2 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 16.0 GB of RAM, and no local storage, designed for lightweight database and caching workloads.

tagline: A memory-optimized virtual server with dedicated Intel Xeon cores and a high memory-to-vCPU ratio for lightweight caching and database workloads.

16.0 GB of system memory providing 8.0 GB per core
No local storage or GPU accelerators included
Delivers 2.52 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks
Best suited for lightweight caching, database operations, and basic web serving
